memory.summary: My project is barely started, but I'd rather grab some leisure time with friends than stress about it now.

desires: Not wake up tomorrow feeling like I let the whole cohort down by dragging them into this awkward post-deadline hangout.
Get a quick, low-stakes win (that trivial chore) done so I can send one last text and fully detach from the 'work' mode before we laugh about everything.
Make sure nobody at the group chat notices I'm actually exhausted and just nodding along to keep the peace.

goals.description: Finish the trivial chore, grab a quick snack with teammate_A, and send a low-effort text to cohort_3 saying 'Hey! Just finished up, ready to vibe.' Then immediately drop all work mode.
goals.rationale: I need to secure that easy win now so I don't look like I'm dragging everyone down. If I let the deadline sit there while we're hanging out, it'll create awkward tension and ruin the mood with my friends. A quick text confirms I'm present and not ghosting, which keeps the peace without me having to explain why I'm tired or struggling. It's an instant win that lets us laugh about everything later.

goals.description: Scroll through memes or do absolutely nothing until Thursday hits hard enough to force me to think about the term project.
goals.rationale: Why stress over Thursday when I can just enjoy tonight? Pushing back on the professor or forcing myself to work feels like extra friction that isn't worth it right now. I'll let the pressure build naturally; if it doesn't happen soon, well, maybe someone else will catch up while I relax.
